$comment: Actio macro layer. This is the ONLY hand-authored schema surface. `scripts/build-schema.mjs` merges it onto the vendored official GitHub Actions workflow schema (vendor/github-workflow.json) to produce actio.schema.json. Edit macros here, then run `npm run schema:build -w actio-core`.

meta.title: Actio workflow (.actio.yml)
meta.description: Source language for the Actio transpiler: the official GitHub Actions workflow schema (inherited wholesale from SchemaStore) plus Actio macro keywords (fragments/inject, retry, dynamic_matrix, fallback, for_each) that compile into standard workflow YAML. Every standard GitHub Actions key, event, and value is validated and autocompleted from the upstream schema; the macro keys are additive. See https://github.com/austenstone/actio.

fragments.title: fragments (Actio macro)
fragments.description: Actio macro. Reusable, named lists of steps. Splice a fragment into a job with `- inject: <name>`. The `fragments` key is stripped from the generated workflow.
fragments.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: reusable, named step lists.

Define ordered step lists once, then splice them into any job with `- inject: <name>`. The `fragments` key is stripped from the generated workflow.

```yaml
fragments:
  setup:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
jobs:
  build:
    steps:
      - inject: setup
      - run: npm ci
```

additionalProperties.description: An ordered list of steps to inject wherever this fragment is referenced.

params.description: Actio macro. Typed compile-time parameters. References are resolved with `{{ params.<name> }}` or `{{ toJSON(params.<name>) }}` and stripped from emitted workflow YAML.

job_defaults.description: Actio macro. Default job-level settings merged into jobs at compile time. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

executors.description: Actio macro. Named runner/container/service presets referenced by `jobs.<id>.executor`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

inject.title: inject (Actio macro)
inject.description: Actio macro. Splice in the steps of the named fragment (defined under top-level `fragments:`) at this position. Replaced inline by `actio build`.
inject.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: splice a fragment here.

Replaced inline by `actio build` with the steps of the named fragment from top-level `fragments:`.

```yaml
steps:
  - inject: setup
  - run: npm test
```

static_if.title: static_if (Actio macro)
static_if.description: Actio macro. Compile-time structural condition. If false, this entire step map is omitted from emitted workflow YAML.
static_if.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: compile-time structural condition.

Evaluated at transpile time against compile-time roots (`params.*`, `for_each.*`, `define.*`). If the expression is false the entire step is removed from the generated workflow; if true the `static_if` key is stripped and the rest is emitted verbatim. Use `if:` for runtime conditions.

```yaml
steps:
  - name: Publish preview
    static_if: params.deploy
    run: ./publish.sh
```

unsafe.title: unsafe (Actio macro)
unsafe.description: Actio macro. Opt this step out of injection-hoist entirely (equivalent to `injectionHoist: off`). Untrusted `${{ }}` interpolations in `run:` are left inline. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.
unsafe.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: opt out of injection-hoist for this step.

Equivalent to `injectionHoist: off`. Untrusted `${{ }}` interpolations in the `run:` body are left inline instead of being hoisted into env vars.

trust.title: trust (Actio macro)
trust.description: Actio macro. Expression path(s) injection-hoist should treat as trusted (never hoist) for this step, e.g. `github.event.issue.title`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

force.title: force (Actio macro)
force.description: Actio macro. Expression path(s) injection-hoist should always hoist for this step even if normally trusted, e.g. `github.sha`. Stripped from generated workflow YAML.

^static_if\\(.+\\)$.description: Actio macro. Conditional merge map. When expression is true, merge this mapping into the parent map.

static_if.title: static_if (Actio macro)
static_if.description: Actio macro. Compile-time structural condition. If false, this entire job map is omitted from emitted workflow YAML.
static_if.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: compile-time structural condition.

Evaluated at transpile time against compile-time roots (`params.*`, `for_each.*`, `define.*`). If the expression is false the entire job is removed from the generated workflow; if true the `static_if` key is stripped and the rest is emitted verbatim. Use `if:` for runtime conditions.

```yaml
jobs:
  release:
    static_if: params.deploy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: ./release.sh
```

executor.description: Actio macro. Name (or ordered list of names) from top-level `executors:`. Expanded during compile and stripped from output.

^static_if\\(.+\\)$.description: Actio macro. Conditional merge map. When expression is true, merge this mapping into the parent map.
Actio macro. Conditional merge map. When expression is true, merge this mapping into the parent map.

injectionHoistMode.title: injectionHoist (Actio macro)
injectionHoistMode.description: Actio macro. Mode for the injection-hoist pass that auto-defuses `${{ }}` script-injection in `run:` bodies. `fix` (default) hoists untrusted interpolations into env vars and rewrites the body to a quoted shell var; `warn`/`error` only diagnose; `off` disables. Settable at root, job, or step level (most specific wins). Stripped from generated workflow YAML.
injectionHoistMode.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: control injection-hoist.

Auto-defuses `${{ }}` script-injection in `run:` bodies by hoisting untrusted interpolations (e.g. `github.event.*`, `github.head_ref`) into generated `env:` vars and rewriting the body to reference a quoted shell variable.

- `fix` (default): hoist and rewrite silently.
- `warn`: diagnose only, leave inline.
- `error`: error only, leave inline.
- `off`: disable for this scope.

Settable at root, job, or step level; the most specific wins.

default.description: JSON-like default value. Use `{{ toJSON(params.<name>) }}` for string interpolation.

retry.title: retry (Actio macro)
retry.description: Actio macro. Retry a flaky step with optional backoff; expands into a chain of conditional attempts where attempt N runs only if attempt N-1 failed. Shorthand `retry: 3` is the same as `retry: { attempts: 3 }`.
retry.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: retry a flaky step.

Expands into a chain of conditional attempts: attempt _N_ runs only if attempt _N-1_ failed. Shorthand `retry: 3` is the same as `retry: { attempts: 3 }`.

```yaml
steps:
  - run: flaky-command
    retry:
      attempts: 3
      delay: 10s
```

delay.description: Backoff between attempts: a duration like "10s", "2m", "1h", or a number of seconds. Injects `sleep` steps between attempts.

fallback.title: fallback (Actio macro)
fallback.description: Actio macro. A native try/catch. Default (notify): the fallback runs on failure via `if: failure()` but the job still fails. With `recover: true` the guarded step gets `continue-on-error: true` and the fallback keys off `outcome`, so the job can pass.
fallback.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: a native try/catch.

- **notify** (default): the fallback runs on failure via `if: failure()`, but the job still fails.
- **recover** (`recover: true`): the guarded step gets `continue-on-error: true` and the fallback keys off `outcome`, so the job can pass.

```yaml
steps:
  - run: deploy
    fallback:
      recover: true
      steps:
        - run: ./rollback.sh
```

oneOf.description: Fallback steps (notify mode). The job still fails.

steps.description: Steps to run when the guarded step or job fails.

recover.description: When true, swallow the guarded failure (continue-on-error) so the job can continue. Step-level only; ignored on job-level fallback.

dynamicMatrix.title: dynamic_matrix (Actio macro)
dynamicMatrix.description: Actio macro. Build a matrix at runtime: `script` prints a JSON array (or `{include:[...]}`) to stdout. Actio generates a setup job that publishes the JSON and wires this job to consume it via `fromJSON()` with a fail-fast:false default and an empty-matrix guard.
dynamicMatrix.markdownDescription:
**Actio macro**: build a matrix at runtime.

`script` prints a JSON array (or `{ include: [...] }`) to stdout. Actio generates a setup job that publishes the JSON and wires this job to consume it via `fromJSON()`, with `fail-fast: false` and an empty-matrix guard by default.

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    dynamic_matrix:
      script: ./list-targets.sh
      alias: target
    steps:
      - run: ./test ${{ matrix.target }}
```

script.description: Command or local script path that prints a JSON array (or `{"include":[...]}`) to stdout.

alias.description: Wrap a scalar JSON array under `matrix.<alias>` (referenced as `${{ matrix.<alias> }}`). Omit for raw `{include:[...]}` mode.

runs-on.description: Runner for the generated setup job. Defaults to the job's `runs-on`, else `ubuntu-latest`.

shell.description: Shell that runs `script` in the setup job, like a step's `shell`. Supported: bash, sh, pwsh, powershell, python. Actio emits matching plumbing to publish the matrix output; other shells (e.g. cmd) are rejected.

compact.description: Pipe the script's stdout through `jq -c .` to normalize the matrix JSON to one line. POSIX-only (bash/sh); ignored for pwsh/powershell/python, which emit raw multi-line output that `fromJSON` parses fine. Defaults to true.

checkout.description: Check out the repository in the setup job. Defaults to true when `script` is a local path.

fail-fast.description: Value for `strategy.fail-fast` on the matrix job. Defaults to false.

id.description: Override the generated setup job id.
